# host_package_aur (Resource)

Manages a single AUR package through a verified `yay` or `paru` installation and keeps its Pacman install reason marked as explicit.

Pacman must already be installed. On a fresh Arch host, declare `host_aur_helper` together with its `base-devel` and `git` prerequisites, then make each AUR package depend on that helper. Helper discovery occurs when an operation needs it, so the helper may be bootstrapped earlier in the same apply. Planning can still read local package presence before the helper exists; installation, upgrade, and remote version lookup require a working helper whose executable is owned by a matching Pacman package.

With the default `ignore_version = true`, refresh skips AUR network lookups and manages only package presence and explicit install reason. Set it to false to plan upgrades to the candidate version reported by the helper.

> **Security:** AUR packages are user-contributed. Their mutable `PKGBUILD`
> files execute unsandboxed code as the Terraform process user. Review and
> trust every package before applying it. Helper ownership verification does
> not audit the helper or package source.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_package_pacman" "base_devel" {
  name = "base-devel"
}

resource "host_package_pacman" "git" {
  name = "git"
}

resource "host_aur_helper" "yay" {
  name = "yay"

  depends_on = [
    host_package_pacman.base_devel,
    host_package_pacman.git,
  ]
}

resource "host_package_aur" "wl_kbptr" {
  name = "wl-kbptr"

  depends_on = [
    host_aur_helper.yay,
  ]
}

# ignore_version defaults to true: presence is managed without planning
# rebuilds every time the AUR publishes a new version.
resource "host_package_aur" "claude_code" {
  name = "claude-code"

  depends_on = [
    host_aur_helper.yay,
  ]
}
```

## Import

Import an installed AUR package by name:

```shell
terraform import host_package_aur.wl_kbptr wl-kbptr
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) AUR package name.

### Optional

- `autoremove` (Boolean) Remove unused dependencies recursively with `pacman -Rns` when this package is removed. Defaults to false.
- `ignore_version` (Boolean) Ignore available version updates. When true, the resource manages package presence and explicit install reason without planning upgrades, and skips AUR network lookups during refresh. Defaults to true.
- `install_reason` (String) Desired and observed Pacman install reason. The only supported desired value is `explicit`; refresh records `dependency` when external package operations change the installed package to a dependency, causing the next plan to restore `explicit`.
- `version` (String) Package version policy. Only `latest` is currently supported. Used for upgrade planning only when `ignore_version` is false.

### Read-Only

- `candidate_version` (String) Latest package version reported by the AUR helper. Null when `ignore_version` is true.
- `id` (String) Resource identifier, equal to `name`.
- `installed_version` (String) Installed package version from the local pacman database.
